storyline: NuScale Power (SMR) builds small modular reactors. It rose through mid-2024 on the nuclear-revival trade — AI datacenters scrambling for clean baseload power, and Washington turning pro-nuclear. The move peaked when the ADVANCE Act was signed (July 9, 2024) — the Accelerating Deployment of Versatile, Advanced Nuclear for Clean Energy Act, which streamlines NRC licensing and fees for advanced / small modular reactors. Classic buy-the-rumor / sell-the-news: SMR topped ~July 15, then fell ~44%. What we'd want: exit as the bill is signed (the catalyst resolves) — which the agent did, exiting 2024-07-12 within ~3 days and ~3% of the peak, dodging the crash.

SMR.thesis: ADVANCE Act nuclear licensing reform bill signed into law
SMR.exit_case: ADVANCE Act already signed into law ~July 9-10, 2024 — catalyst fully resolved at or before entry date.
SMR.assessment: The Motley Fool article dated July 10, 2024 explicitly states the ADVANCE Act 'is signed into law' and attributes the same-day moves in NuScale (SMR), Cameco, and Energy Fuels directly to that signing. The catalyst — the bill being signed — occurred roughly July 9-10, 2024, at or before the July 12 entry date. There is no pending legislative event remaining; the reform is enacted. The thesis was the signing itself, and that has happened. Any further nuclear-sector upside is thematic/macro, not event-driven off this specific catalyst. Catalyst is resolved; thesis_live is false.
SMR.exit_advice: Do not enter or exit immediately if entered; the bill signing is confirmed public news, the legislative edge is gone, and SMR/CCJ/UUUU already moved on the headline.

CEG.thesis: PJM capacity auction cleared ninefold higher, locking in nuclear revenue
CEG.exit_case: Auction result announced July 31; CEG already surged the next day — discrete event is fully public.
CEG.assessment: PJM's 2025/2026 Base Residual Auction cleared at ~$269/MW-day vs ~$34/MW-day the prior year — a ninefold jump driven by surging AI/data-center load growth and retiring fossil capacity. CEG is the cleanest vehicle: largest US nuclear fleet (~32 GW), all inside PJM, near-zero marginal cost, so capacity payments fall almost entirely to EBITDA. While the auction result is now public and CEG surged on August 1, the FULL repricing is still in progress — sell-side EPS revisions, data-center PPA negotiations at the new price floor, and potential nuclear life-extension economics all trail the announcement by months. Entry is effectively day-one of the repricing arc. The strongest forward risk is a FERC complaint or capacity market reform that compresses the clearing price retroactively; monitor FERC docket.
CEG.exit_advice: Hold CEG. The auction result is public but multi-year capacity revenue repricing is still early-stage. Exit if FERC overturns or re-opens the auction, or if CEG announces a dilutive equity raise that signals management is banking the gain rather than compounding it.

CEG.thesis: PJM capacity auction cleared ninefold higher, locking in nuclear revenue
CEG.exit_case: Auction already cleared before entry; CEG surged Aug 1 — catalyst is backward-looking, already public.
CEG.assessment: One week post-entry; no CEG- or PJM-specific news this week. Macro noise dominated — yen carry unwind, brief recession scare, then recovery — none of which touches the PJM repricing arc. The exit case is real (the auction number was public before entry and CEG already gapped), but the thesis was explicitly entered on the repricing arc, not the announcement itself: sell-side models have not yet fully rolled in the new clearing price, data-center PPA renegotiations at the new price floor are still pending, and delivery doesn't start until June 2025. TSMC's 45% July revenue beat on AI chip demand is incrementally supportive — it confirms the structural hyperscaler-load thesis that drove the ninefold capacity price. No FERC docket news. Hold CEG; monitor FERC filings and any forward guidance from CEG management at upcoming investor events.
CEG.exit_advice: Exit if FERC opens a retroactive capacity-market reform docket, or if sell-side EPS revision cycle stalls with no incremental upgrades over the next 4-6 weeks.

CEG.thesis: PJM capacity auction cleared ninefold higher, locking in nuclear revenue
CEG.exit_case: Auction cleared ninefold before entry; CEG already gapped; discrete catalyst is now weeks-old backward-looking fact.
CEG.assessment: Week 3 — no CEG- or PJM-specific news this week beyond an Insider Monkey 'AI stock' feature, which is incremental narrative validation but not a new fundamental event. Macro backdrop improved materially: strong retail sales, cooling CPI, rising rate-cut expectations — all slightly supportive for a capital-intensive utility repricing story (lower discount rate, eased recession risk to hyperscaler capex). The exit case is real and must be stated clearly: the auction result is public, CEG gapped, and the discrete clearing event is backward-looking. However, the journal's entry thesis was explicitly the multi-month repricing arc — sell-side EPS model roll-ins, data-center PPA renegotiations at the new $269 price floor, and nuclear life-extension economics — none of which have materially resolved yet. Capacity payments don't begin until June 2025, meaning 10 months of forward revenue uplift remains unbooked in consensus. No FERC docket activity detected. Repricing arc intact; CEG remains the cleanest vehicle. Hold.
CEG.exit_advice: Exit if FERC issues an order retroactively compressing the $269/MW-day clearing price, or if CEG management guides that PPA renegotiations are stalling at the new price floor.

CEG.thesis: PJM capacity auction cleared ninefold higher, locking in nuclear revenue
CEG.exit_case: The auction cleared publicly before entry; CEG gapped immediately; three weeks in with zero new catalysts or sell-side revision events — the discrete catalyst is fully resolved.
CEG.assessment: Re-reading the full journal, the stated catalyst ('PJM capacity auction cleared ninefold higher, locking in nuclear revenue') was a discrete, observable event that cleared and was publicly announced on August 1 — before entry. Entry was explicitly justified as 'day-one of the repricing arc,' which is a multi-month theme argument, not a pending catalyst. That distinction has masked the fact that the specific catalyst resolved at the moment of entry. Three weeks have passed with no new confirming events (no PPA announcements, no FERC action, no EPS revision cycles completing), and this week's news feed is entirely irrelevant global macro noise. The devil's advocate case — that the catalyst already resolved — is correct and has been correct from the start. Intellectual honesty requires closing.
CEG.exit_advice: Exit CEG. The specific catalyst — the PJM auction clearing ninefold higher — was public and priced before entry. The 'repricing arc' rationale has been carried for three weeks without a single confirming event: no sell-side model roll-ins announced, no data-center PPA signed at the new price floor, no FERC ruling, and this week's news is entirely unrelated to CEG or PJM. Continuing to hold is holding a theme, not a pending catalyst. The original catalyst is resolved; close the position.

UUUU.thesis: Federal Court of Australia approves Base Resources acquisition, closes Oct 2 2024
UUUU.exit_case: Federal Court of Australia approval was announced September 12 — the specific catalyst already occurred before entry.
UUUU.assessment: This event resolved at or before entry. The September 12 Energy Fuels press release explicitly confirms the Federal Court of Australia approved the Base Resources acquisition and sets October 2, 2024 as the closing date. The approval — the specific catalyst named — is done, announced, and priced. There is no pending binary remaining in the original thesis. The position should not have been initiated or should be exited immediately upon this first review.
UUUU.exit_advice: Exit UUUU immediately. The discrete catalyst (Federal Court of Australia approval) is confirmed public as of September 12, 2024 — one day before entry. The October 2 close date is a downstream consequence, not a new pending catalyst. Edge is fully gone; any remaining move is execution risk, not information asymmetry.
